# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains architectural claims, benchmark results, ablation results, efficiency claims for a specialized context manager, test-time scaling tradeoffs, and stated study limitations for a long-horizon LLM agent framework.

## Evidence Files

### file: evidence-01.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-005
evidence_id: evidence-01
kind: method
citation: Wan et al., 2025, Abstract
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.08790
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
COMPASS is a hierarchical framework with three specialized components: a MainAgent, a Meta-Thinker, and a ContextManager.

## Source Excerpt

"We propose COMPASS (Context-Organized Multi-Agent Planning and Strategy System), a lightweight hierarchical framework that separates tactical execution, strategic oversight, and context organization into three specialized components: (1) a MainAgent ... (2) a Meta-Thinker ... and (3) a ContextManager ..."

## Note

Direct architectural description from the abstract.
```

### file: evidence-02.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-005
evidence_id: evidence-02
kind: claim-support
citation: Wan et al., 2025, Section 2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.08790
confidence: medium
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The paper identifies dynamic context growth as the main bottleneck in long-horizon tasks because execution traces can exceed the model's finite context window and obscure earlier information.

## Source Excerpt

"The principal challenge lies in managing the dynamic context: as the execution trace grows—often linearly with time, |Cdyn_t| / O(t)—it can exceed the model's finite context window, obscuring earlier but potentially essential information."

## Note

This is the paper's stated problem framing and mechanism hypothesis, not an independently established causal proof.
```

### file: evidence-03.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-005
evidence_id: evidence-03
kind: method
citation: Wan et al., 2025, Section 3.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.08790
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The Meta-Thinker monitors the MainAgent asynchronously and can issue strategic signals such as reflection, termination, or verification when it detects anomalies or signs of completion.

## Source Excerpt

"It remains silent until anomalies are detected—such as looping behavior, tool misuse, or signs of task completion. Once triggered, it issues a high-level strategic signal to prompt reflection, termination, or verification ... Running asynchronously ensures that these interventions do not block the MainAgent's reasoning ..."

## Note

Direct role description of the Meta-Thinker.
```

### file: evidence-04.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-005
evidence_id: evidence-04
kind: method
citation: Wan et al., 2025, Section 3.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.08790
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The ContextManager builds a refreshed task-specific context by combining persistent notes, the current reasoning trajectory, and the Meta-Thinker's strategic signal.

## Source Excerpt

"It synthesizes a new, task-specific context by selectively drawing from three sources: (i) the persistent structured notes accumulated across turns, (ii) the current reasoning trajectory from MainAgent, and (iii) the Meta-Thinker's strategic signal."

## Note

Direct role description of the ContextManager.
```

### file: evidence-05.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-005
evidence_id: evidence-05
kind: metric
citation: Wan et al., 2025, Table 2, Gemini 2.5 Pro
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.08790
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
On BrowseComp with Gemini 2.5 Pro, COMPASS achieved 35.4 Pass@1 versus 29.8 for the single-agent + context variant and 31.8 for the agent-as-a-tool multi-agent baseline.

## Source Excerpt

Table 2 reports for Gemini 2.5 Pro: "+Context" on BrowseComp = 29.8, "Agent-as-a-Tool" = 31.8, and "COMPASS" = 35.4.

## Note

This is a benchmark result on one dataset and model configuration.
```

### file: evidence-06.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-005
evidence_id: evidence-06
kind: metric
citation: Wan et al., 2025, Table 2, Gemini 2.5 Pro
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.08790
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
On GAIA and HLE with Gemini 2.5 Pro, COMPASS scored 67.8 and 31.7 Pass@1, compared with 63.1 and 28.4 for the single-agent + context variant.

## Source Excerpt

Table 2 reports for Gemini 2.5 Pro: "+Context" = 63.1 on GAIA and 28.4 on HLE, while "COMPASS" = 67.8 on GAIA and 31.7 on HLE.

## Note

This unit isolates the reported gains on the other two primary benchmarks.
```

### file: evidence-07.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-005
evidence_id: evidence-07
kind: metric
citation: Wan et al., 2025, Table 3, Meta-Thinker ablation
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.08790
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
Removing the Meta-Thinker reduced BrowseComp Pass@1 from 35.4 to 15.2 while leaving PAR high at 0.92 and PVR low at 0.12.

## Source Excerpt

Table 3, "Reference: Full System" reports Pass@1 = 35.4, PAR = 0.85, PVR = 0.48. Under "Ablation 1: Meta-Thinking Agent ... None (Main+Context only)" the table reports Pass@1 = 15.2, PAR = 0.92, PVR = 0.12.

## Note

The high persistence score alongside low pivot recognition is presented as a failure mode rather than a pure improvement.
```

### file: evidence-08.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-005
evidence_id: evidence-08
kind: metric
citation: Wan et al., 2025, Table 3, ContextManager ablation
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.08790
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
Removing the ContextManager reduced BrowseComp Pass@1 from 35.4 to 26.4 and changed strategic metrics to PAR 0.91 and PVR 0.18.

## Source Excerpt

Table 3 reports "Gemini2.5Pro (Full System)" with Pass@1 = 35.4, PAR = 0.85, PVR = 0.48. Under "Ablation 2: ContextManager ... None (No ContextManager)" it reports Pass@1 = 26.4, PAR = 0.91, PVR = 0.18.

## Note

The ablation shifts metrics in a mixed way, indicating a tradeoff rather than a uniformly degraded profile on every strategic measure.
```

### file: evidence-09.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-005
evidence_id: evidence-09
kind: metric
citation: Wan et al., 2025, Section 5.1 and Figure 4
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.08790
confidence: medium
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The authors report that Context-12B achieved performance comparable to Gemini 2.5 Flash on BrowseComp while using 70% of its tokens after SFT-DPO training.

## Source Excerpt

"As shown in Figure 4, we evaluated the performance of Context-12B on Browsecomp, achieving performance comparable to a larger models (Gemini2.5Flash) while using only 70% of their tokens with a SFT-DPO training pipeline."

## Note

This claim is reported narratively in the paper text; the exact comparable accuracy value is not stated in the excerpted sentence.
```

### file: evidence-10.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-005
evidence_id: evidence-10
kind: incident
citation: Wan et al., 2025, Limitations
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.08790
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The study's evaluation is limited to controlled QA-style settings with searching, text-based browsing, and code execution, and it primarily uses proprietary frontier models.

## Source Excerpt

"While COMPASS demonstrates strong performance on QA-style agentic benchmarks, our evaluation currently emphasizes controlled reasoning environments ..." and "Specifically, experiments are conducted within settings that involve searching, text-based browsing, and code execution tools." The paper also states: "Our study primarily focuses on proprietary frontier models ..."

## Note

This limitation constrains how directly the reported results transfer to more open-ended, real-world, or open-model settings.
---

COMPASS is a hierarchical framework with three specialized components: a MainAgent, a Meta-Thinker, and a ContextManager.

The paper identifies dynamic context growth as the main bottleneck in long-horizon tasks because execution traces can exceed the model's finite context window and obscure earlier information.

## Source Excerpt

"The principal challenge lies in managing the dynamic context: as the execution trace grows—often linearly with time, |Cdyn_t| / O(t)—it can exceed the model's finite context window, obscuring earlier but potentially essential information."

## Note

This is the paper's stated problem framing and mechanism hypothesis, not an independently established causal proof.

The Meta-Thinker monitors the MainAgent asynchronously and can issue strategic signals such as reflection, termination, or verification when it detects anomalies or signs of completion.

## Source Excerpt

"It remains silent until anomalies are detected—such as looping behavior, tool misuse, or signs of task completion. Once triggered, it issues a high-level strategic signal to prompt reflection, termination, or verification ... Running asynchronously ensures that these interventions do not block the MainAgent's reasoning ..."

## Note

Direct role description of the Meta-Thinker.
```

The ContextManager builds a refreshed task-specific context by combining persistent notes, the current reasoning trajectory, and the Meta-Thinker's strategic signal.

"It synthesizes a new, task-specific context by selectively drawing from three sources: (i) the persistent structured notes accumulated across turns, (ii) the current reasoning trajectory from MainAgent, and (iii) the Meta-Thinker's strategic signal."

Direct role description of the ContextManager.

The high persistence score alongside low pivot recognition is presented as a failure mode rather than a pure improvement.

## Note
